title: go routine panics while doing podman pull
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

I changed my ansible playbook to run containers as a user instead of root. Now go segfaults at the very first command, image pull.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Ansible does this:

```podman pull homeassistant/home-assistant:stable```


**Describe the results you received:**

See Ansible debug log:
```
TASK [podman_container_systemd : running single container, ensure we have up to date container image] *************************************************************************************************************
task path: /home/itengval/src/podman-container-systemd/tasks/main.yml:67
<localhost> ESTABLISH LOCAL CONNECTION FOR USER: itengval
<localhost> EXEC /bin/sh -c 'echo ~itengval && sleep 0'
<localhost> EXEC /bin/sh -c '( umask 77 && mkdir -p "` echo /var/tmp/ansible-tmp-1584713703.8163428-185661084491341 `" && echo ansible-tmp-1584713703.8163428-185661084491341="` echo /var/tmp/ansible-tmp-1584713703.8163428-185661084491341 `" ) && sleep 0'
Using module file /usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/ansible/modules/commands/command.py
<localhost> PUT /home/itengval/.ansible/tmp/ansible-local-3155378u5q49l/tmpxz7ugd2v TO /var/tmp/ansible-tmp-1584713703.8163428-185661084491341/AnsiballZ_command.py
<localhost> EXEC /bin/sh -c 'setfacl -m u:redhatter:r-x /var/tmp/ansible-tmp-1584713703.8163428-185661084491341/ /var/tmp/ansible-tmp-1584713703.8163428-185661084491341/AnsiballZ_command.py && sleep 0'
<localhost> EXEC /bin/sh -c 'sudo -H -S -n  -u redhatter /bin/sh -c '"'"'echo BECOME-SUCCESS-ubufzsmqiqzbckrszwxmnjtyetjmavus ; /usr/libexec/platform-python /var/tmp/ansible-tmp-1584713703.8163428-185661084491341/AnsiballZ_command.py'"'"' && sleep 0'
<localhost> EXEC /bin/sh -c 'rm -f -r /var/tmp/ansible-tmp-1584713703.8163428-185661084491341/ > /dev/null 2>&1 && sleep 0'
```

```
  - name: running single container, ensure we have up to date container image
    command: "podman pull {{ container_image }}"
    become_user: "{{ container_run_as_user }}"
    when: container_image is defined
```

```
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 3
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: {}
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 18
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.6.4-2.module+el8.1.1+5363+bf8ff1af.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
rhel8.1 laptop

comments.body:
ah, I got some good tip from IRC:

I tried to take the debug info for the above as a user, which failed:
```
[root@gr8 ~]# su redhatter  -c "podman info --debug"
cannot chdir: Permission denied
```

Which them gave a lead:
```
16:29:16 | <mheon> | ikkeT: rootless podman + sudo is uh... iffy. we use a lot of systemd things if systemd is available. and systemd does not understand or appreciate su and sudo
-- | -- | --
16:29:22 | <mheon> | so it doesn't make /run/use/$UID
16:29:28 | <mheon> | and we don't have a temporary files dir
16:29:34 | <mheon> | if sudo is involved
16:29:48 | <ikkeT> | hmmm... then ansible won't ever work?
16:30:43 | <ikkeT> | i need to think a bit what to do then

```

So perhaps erroring out with eroor message instead of panic if such temp dir is missing?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I suggest waiting for a newer Podman in RHEL. Note that RHEL issues should be filed through the Red Hat channels.
This should be a bugzilla, currently works in Master/Podman 1.9
